title: Google Chrome OS video
body: They have officially presented the [Google](http://www.xarop.com/category/google/) Operating System for [netbooks](http://www.miminipc.com "ultraportables, netbooks, mini computers"). At first glance, Google Chrome OS could be defined as a remote desktop, well, if it is an Operating System because it only starts our computer in a few seconds, but everything we see on our computer and desktop is really in Google, the applications and our documents. There are many advantages, we do not depend on our computer, if we lose it or it breaks we do not lose our documents and settings, we simply have to install another Chrome OS and log in, and we will have everything again. To achieve this we have to be permanently connected to the internet, something we are already getting used to with our mobile phones, iPhones, Androids, etc. In this video Google explains to us in a fun, and very commercial way, what it is about. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0QRO3gKj3qw
